title: Phrasal verbs 1
explanation:
Phrasal verbs

A phrasal verb = verb + particle (up, out, off, on…). The meaning is often different from the main verb alone.

  • get up — leave your bed
  • turn on / off — start / stop a machine
  • look after — take care of
  • find out — discover information
  • pick up — collect someone/something

questions.text: Please [*turn off|turn on|turn up] the lights when you leave.
I [*get up|get on|get off] at seven every morning.
Can you [*look after|look for|look at] my cat this weekend?
I need to [*find out|find up|find off] what time the train leaves.
She [*picked up|picked off|picked on] her children from school.
[*Put on|Put off|Put up] your coat — it's cold outside.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: Don't ____ — keep trying! | *give up | give in | give on
I'm ____ my keys. Have you seen them? | *looking for | looking after | looking up
We need to ____ early tomorrow. | *set off | set up | set on
He ____ smoking last year. | *gave up | gave in | gave off
The meeting was [*put off|put on|put up] until Friday.
I [*looked up|looked after|looked for] the word in a dictionary.
She [*took off|took on|took up] her jacket because it was warm.
We [*ran out of|ran off|ran up] milk, so I went to the shop.
questions.feedback: Run out of = have none left.

questions.text: He [*grew up|grew on|grew off] in a small village.
